channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tren de Aragua members arrested in Texas raid
item.description: The raid saw multiple Tren de Aragua members arrested after complaints alleging human trafficking and narcotic violations at the complex.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants in Gaza fire rockets into Israel as it marks Oct. 7
item.description: Palestinian militants in Gaza fired a barrage of rockets into Israel on Monday as mourners marked the anniversary of the Oct. 7 attack, without disrupting a nearby ceremony. Lebanon’s Health Ministry said an Israeli strike in the country’s south killed at least 10 firefighters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: A leading newspaper in North Carolina is blasting former President Donald Trump over what it calls his "falsehoods" about the government response to Hurricane Helene, which devastated large swaths of the state last week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KREX 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton is forecast to become a Category 5 and is taking aim at Florida
item.description: Milton rapidly strengthened in the Gulf of Mexico on Monday and was forecast to become a Category 5 storm on a path toward Florida, threatening a dangerous storm surge in Tampa Bay and setting the stage for potential mass evacuations less than two weeks after a catastrophic Hurricane Helene swamped
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tossed balls from stands, apparently aimed at Profar, interrupt Padres' win in NLDS Game 2
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tempers flared on the field and in the stands at Dodger Stadium, with rowdy fans tossing baseballs, apparently at San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar, and then trash that caused a 12-minute wait between pitches during the seventh inning of the Padres' 10-2 win in NL Division S
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court declines Biden administration appeal in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday let stand a decision barring emergency abortions that violate the law in Texas, which has one of the country's strictest abortion bans.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump and Harris mark somber anniversary of Hamas’ Oct. 7 attack on Israel
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Former President Donald Trump and Vice President Kamala Harris are marking the first anniversary of the deadliest attack on Jews since the Holocaust as the presidential candidates approach the final weeks of the campaign during a widening conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris' and Donald Trump's economic plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Miss Teen Rodeo Kansas, 19, killed in car crash; teammates injured
item.description: Emma Brungardt, 19, was killed in a single-vehicle crash that left several of her teammates injured on Friday in rural Thomas County, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tips, overtime, Social Security: A look at Donald Trump's no-tax pledges and what they might cost
item.description: Donald Trump has pledged to end taxes on everything from tips to Social Security and overtime pay if he's elected to the White House again. But he hasn't detailed how he would fund those ideas and avoid creating a huge budget shortfall, beyond arguing he will usher in an economic boom.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colleges brace for Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: Colleges face a major test Monday as pro-Palestinian and pro-Israel students both hold events for the first anniversary of Hamas’s attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US spends a record $17.9 billion on military aid to Israel since last Oct. 7
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States has spent a record of at least $17.9 billion on military aid to Israel since the war in Gaza began and led to escalating conflict around the Middle East, according to a report for Brown University's Costs of War project, released Monday on the anniversary of Hamas
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deadliest attack on Jews since the Holocaust hits one-year mark and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court taking the bench with ghost guns, a capital case and transgender rights on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is taking the bench again on Monday, ready to hear cases on ghost guns, a death sentence and transgender rights.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton explodes to Category 4 as Florida prepares for massive evacuations
item.description: Milton didn't spend much time as a Category 3 hurricane Monday morning before rapidly growing to a Category 4.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey high school allegedly banned yellow ribbons honoring Israeli hostages: 'Deeply offensive'
item.description: Yellow ribbons meant to honor Israeli hostages abducted by Hamas were allegedly banned at a club fair, which critics called "deeply offensive" and "blatant antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former model recalls Jeffrey Epstein abuse at private island, speaks out after his death: 'My life spiraled'
item.description: Lisa Phillips is a former model who said late financier Jeffrey Epstein abused her at his private island. The convicted sex offender took his life in 2019 while he was behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee man smashes gas pumps, throws rock at window after he was denied access to bathroom, police say
item.description: A man is accused of throwing a rock at a window and smashing gas pumps at a gas station in Memphis, Tennessee, after he was denied access to the bathroom.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US court to review civil rights lawsuit alleging environmental racism in a Louisiana parish
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A federal appellate court is set to hear oral arguments Monday in a civil rights lawsuit alleging a south Louisiana parish engaged in racist land-use policies to place polluting industries in majority-Black communities.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Turning Point wants to revolutionize how Republicans turn out voters. Some are skeptical
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Turning Point’s representatives have made two things clear in meetings with state and local Republican leaders — Donald Trump has blessed their conservative organization to help lead his get-out-the-vote effort, and local party officials ought to use the group's new voter mobilizat
item.dc:creator: DAN MERICA and BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New film documents Oct. 7 attacks after first anniversary
item.description: Pierre Rehov, speaking from Ashdod, Israel, said he was disturbed by reactions that seemed to excuse the attack.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: GOP looks to avoid disaster in Nebraska Senate race
item.description: Republicans are looking to avoid a disaster in the Nebraska Senate race amid signs that an independent candidate could be giving incumbent Sen. Deb Fischer (R) a run for her money.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Milton is growing stronger as it blows toward Florida's Tampa Bay region
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Hurricane Milton quickly intensified Sunday and is on track to become a major hurricane with the Tampa Bay area in its sights, putting Florida on edge and triggering evacuation orders along a coast still reeling from Helene’s devastation.
item.dc:creator: FREIDA FRISARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nibi the 'diva' beaver won't be released, Massachusetts governor decides
item.description: The question of whether a 2-year-old beaver named Nibi can stay with the rescuers she has known since she was a baby or must be released into the wild has been resolved, thanks to the governor of Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton growing stronger as it approaches Florida: NHC
item.description: Milton has strengthed into a hurricane in the Gulf of Mexico, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KTLA 5

item.title: Here's what Spirit Halloween seeks in an 'ideal' retail location
item.description: Spirit Halloween made headlines earlier this week after firing back at "SNL" over a sketch that mocked the company's choice of retail locations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Can you stop calls and texts from political campaigns?
item.description: If you've received a political text or call recently, you may be wondering how to make it stop, or what you can do to prevent them in the future.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Milton growing stronger as it approaches Florida: NHC
item.description: Milton has strengthed into a hurricane in the Gulf of Mexico, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teens arrested in NYC for attack on former NY Gov. David Paterson, his stepson
item.description: Two teens have been arrested and charged with gang assault for the attack on former New York Gov. David Paterson and his 20-year-old stepson, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump returns to site of attempted assassination, honors shooting victims
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohtani homers in playoff debut and the Dodgers rally to beat the Padres 7-5 in NLDS opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani hit a three-run homer in his postseason debut, Teoscar Hernández’s two-run single gave Los Angeles its first lead in a playoff game in two years, and the Dodgers beat the San Diego Padres 7-5 in their NL Division Series opener Saturday.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: ROME (AP) — Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands of pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in major European cities and around the globe Saturday to call for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Vanderbilt takes down No. 1 Alabama 40-35 in historic college football victory
item.description: Fans of the Vanderbilt Commodores danced onto the field, jumping up and down as they tore down a goal post, carried it out of the stadium and rang out sweet sounds of victory.
item.dc:creator: TERESA M. WALKER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Death toll from Hurricane Helene rises to 227 as grim task of recovering bodies continues
item.description: The death toll from Hurricane Helene inched up to 227 on Saturday as the grim task of recovering bodies continued more than a week after the monster storm ravaged the Southeast and killed people in six states.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gary police, FBI execute search warrant at home of missing girl; family speaks out
item.description: The City of Gary said Saturday it is "deeply concerned" about a case involving a missing 13-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: Chances increase for tropical development in the Gulf
item.description: Hurricane Kirk is expected to strengthen bringing swells to the U.S. east coast on Sunday, the National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC
